title: trifolium pratense proles sativum
intro: Trifolium pratense proles sativum, also known as red clover, is a type of legume plant that is a subspecies of the white clover. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of red clover include relief from menopausal symptoms, improvement in cardiovascular health, and potential protection against certain types of cancer. Red clover is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
